operating_state: Net sales increased 9.8% to $36.8 billion in Q3 2021, driven by 6.1% comparable sales growth reflecting 12.7% higher average ticket partially offset by 5.8% decline in customer transactions. The average ticket increase stemmed from inflation across product categories including copper and building materials, offset by lumber deflation, plus increased big-ticket transactions and strong demand for innovative products. Online sales represented 12.8% of total sales with 8.4% growth as customers continued leveraging digital platforms.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed slightly to 34.1% from 34.2% due to higher transportation costs and unfavorable product mix, partially offset by higher retail prices. SG&A improved to 16.8% of sales from 18.1% driven by positive comparable sales leverage and cycling $413 million of COVID-19 expenses from prior year, partially offset by wage investments made in late fiscal 2020. Current period COVID-19 expenses totaled $35 million.
material_events: The company issued $3.0 billion of senior notes in September 2021 for general corporate purposes including share repurchases. Board approved new $20.0 billion share repurchase authorization in May 2021 replacing previous authorization, with $14.1 billion remaining available. Helena Foulkes resigned from the Board of Directors in October 2021, and Mark Holifield, Executive Vice President of Supply Chain and Product Development, resigned effective October 31, 2021.
macro_exposures: No material changes in risk factors were disclosed during the period.
forward_outlook: Management anticipates capital expenditures of approximately 2% of net sales annually while maintaining disciplined capital allocation approach. The company plans to continue share repurchases subject to market conditions and business considerations, with amounts influenced by evolving economic environment. Management expects to renew the 364-day $1.0 billion credit facility scheduled to expire in December 2021.
uncertainty_profile: Working capital remains subject to seasonality, inventory management, vendor payment timing, and foreign exchange fluctuations. The company maintains exposure to commodity price inflation and transportation cost pressures. Share repurchase timing and amounts remain contingent on market conditions and evolving economic environment.
